Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild renal impairment (elevated creatinine), hypoglycemia, low HbA1c, metabolic acidosis (low CO₂), and significant proteinuria with ketonuria. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has abnormal odor, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Findings suggest possible acute or chronic kidney dysfunction, poor glycemic control, and possible dehydration or catabolic state. Clinical correlation and further evaluation are recommended.
